skill.name: Form Conversion Rate Optimization (Form CRO)
skill.description: Optimize any form that is NOT signup or account registration—including lead capture, contact, demo request, application, survey, quote, and checkout forms. Use when the goal is to increase form completion rate, reduce friction, or improve lead quality without breaking compliance or downstream workflows.
skill.summary: Expert framework for optimizing form completion rates through a structured health index and field-level best practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. The static analysis found zero files and zero lines of code, resulting in a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides conversion rate optimization guidance through structured prompts and frameworks. No security issues detected—the skill contains only best practices for form optimization with no network calls, file system access, or external command execution.

content.value_statement: Forms with poor design lose up to 50% of potential leads. This skill provides a structured Form Health Index framework to diagnose conversion barriers and deliver actionable optimizations that preserve data quality while reducing user friction.
actual_capabilities: Calculate a Form Health & Friction Index score (0-100) based on six weighted categories including field necessity, cognitive load, and mobile usability
Provide field-level optimization recommendations for common form inputs like email, phone, name, and free-text fields
Design multi-step form flows with progress indicators and logical field ordering
Create inline validation rules and human-readable error messages
Establish measurement frameworks to track form view-to-completion metrics
Optimize submit button copy and trust signals to increase conversion confidence
limitations: Does not apply to signup or account registration forms (use signup-flow-cro skill instead)
Cannot access actual form code or analytics without explicit user input
Does not test or implement changes—this skill provides recommendations only
Does not account for legal compliance requirements which must be verified separately

use_cases.title: Lead Capture Form Audit
use_cases.description: Evaluate and optimize contact forms on landing pages to increase lead volume without sacrificing lead quality.
use_cases.target_user: Growth marketers and landing page designers

use_cases.title: Multi-Step Application Flow
use_cases.description: Design and optimize multi-step forms for job applications, loan applications, or event registrations to reduce abandonment.

use_cases.title: Checkout Process Optimization
use_cases.description: Streamline checkout forms to reduce cart abandonment while maintaining necessary billing and shipping data collection.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce managers and conversion rate specialists

prompt_templates.title: Basic Form Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this form for conversion optimization opportunities. First, calculate the Form Health & Friction Index using these categories: Field Necessity & Efficiency (30), Value-Effort Balance (20), Cognitive Load & Clarity (20), Error Handling & Recovery (15), Trust & Friction Reduction (10), Mobile Usability (5). Form details: [describe form fields, purpose, and current issues]
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a quick assessment of a form's conversion potential

prompt_templates.title: Field-Level Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Provide specific optimization recommendations for these form fields: [list field types, current labels, and any known issues]. For each field, address: necessary vs optional status, best input type, validation rules, mobile considerations, and copy improvements.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants detailed guidance on improving specific form fields

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Form Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multi-step form flow for [describe form purpose and fields]. Include: optimal number of steps, field distribution per step, progress indicator design, back-navigation handling, and save-progress options. Justify each decision based on cognitive load principles.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to design a new multi-step form or restructure an existing long form

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these current error messages and validation rules: [paste current error messages]. Recommend improvements for clarity, actionability, and user-friendliness. Provide specific rewritten error messages for each case.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to improve form error messages to reduce abandonment

output_examples.input: I have a contact form with name, email, phone, company, and message fields. Our completion rate is only 35%. Help me understand why and what to fix.
output_examples.output:
Form Health Index Analysis:

**Score: 62/100 - Conversion-Limited**

Primary Issues Identified:
1. **Field Overload** - 5 fields likely causing 10-25% drop-off. Phone and company should be optional unless operationally critical.
2. **Cognitive Load** - Putting message (high effort) before commitment-building fields increases early abandonment.
3. **Field Order** - Start with easiest fields (name, email) to build momentum.

Recommended Field Order: Name → Email → Company (optional) → Phone (optional) → Message

Priority Fixes: Make phone/company optional with clear 'why' language, reorder fields, add inline email validation with typo correction.

output_examples.input: Our demo request form has 12 fields and our completion rate is only 12%. Should we just remove half the fields?
output_examples.output:
Before removing fields, calculate your Form Health Index. With 12 fields, you're likely in the -25-50% penalty zone, but blind removal risks losing critical qualification data.

Recommended Approach:
1. **Audit field usage** - Which fields actually get acted upon? If company size is collected but never used, it's friction, not value.
2. **Consider multi-step** - 12 fields can work if split into logical phases: Contact Info → Qualification → Details
3. **Score-based recommendation**: If Index shows <55 (Broken), structural fixes first. If 55-69, field optimization with clear justification for each required field.

Do NOT remove fields without understanding business impact. The answer is usually restructuring and making optional, not deletion.

best_practices: Always calculate the Form Health Index before making changes—this provides a baseline for measuring improvement and prevents blind redesigns.
Start form field order with lowest-effort inputs (email, name) to build completion momentum before asking for commitment.
Make fields optional unless there is a clear operational or legal requirement—every required field reduces completion by 5-10%.
anti_patterns: Removing fields without understanding their business purpose—this risks losing qualification data that drives downstream sales.
Using placeholder text as labels—this creates accessibility issues and causes data loss when users start typing.
Validating on every keystroke—this creates frustration; validate after field interaction, not during typing.

faq.question: Does this skill work for signup and registration forms?
faq.answer: No. This skill explicitly excludes signup and account registration forms. Use the signup-flow-cro skill for those form types instead.

faq.question: How long does it take to see improvement after implementing recommendations?
faq.answer: Form optimization is iterative. Expect 2-4 weeks of data collection before reliable conclusions. Small improvements (5-15%) often appear within 1-2 weeks for high-traffic forms.

faq.question: Can this skill help with GDPR and privacy compliance?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides UX optimization guidance only. Privacy compliance requirements must be verified separately by legal or compliance teams.

faq.question: What if our form has legal fields that cannot be removed?
faq.answer: Legal and compliance-required fields should never be removed. This skill helps you optimize around them—making them less prominent, grouping them logically, and ensuring the rest of the form flows smoothly.

faq.question: How does this skill handle mobile-specific optimization?
faq.answer: The Form Health Index includes a 5-point Mobile Usability category. Recommendations address touch target sizes (44px minimum), correct keyboard types, autofill support, and single-column layouts.

faq.question: What metrics should I track after implementing changes?
faq.answer: Track: form view-to-start rate, start-to-completion rate, field-level drop-off, validation error rate by field, time to complete, and device split. This skill's measurement framework provides detailed guidance.
